name: DOMINOS PIZZA INC
business: Domino's is the largest pizza company in the world, operating over 22,100 locations in more than 90 markets. It generates revenues by charging royalties and fees to franchisees, selling food and other products through its supply chain operations, and operating Company-owned stores.
sellsTo: end consumers via delivery and carryout

exposures.sentence: We have been selling quality, affordable food to our customers since 1960.

In the U.S., Domino's generated more than 85% of U.S. retail sales in 2025 from digital channels, and our emphasis on technological innovation has allowed us to develop many innovative ordering platforms.
We have multinational agreements with Uber Technologies, Inc. and DoorDash, Inc. to allow customers to order Domino's products through their respective marketplaces.
Our supply chain segment leases a fleet of more than 1,100 tractors and trailers.
Labor costs are largely a function of the minimum wage for a majority of our store personnel and certain supply chain personnel.

catalysts.event: Launch of Parmesan Stuffed Crust Pizza, Spicy Chicken Bacon Ranch specialty pizza, and Garlic and Cinnamon Bread Bites
catalysts.sentence: During 2025, we launched our Parmesan Stuffed Crust Pizza, as well as our Spicy Chicken Bacon Ranch specialty pizza and Garlic and Cinnamon Bread Bites.

catalysts.event: Full launch of website and mobile web portion of eCommerce redesign
catalysts.sentence: As of December 28, 2025, we have fully launched the website and mobile web portion of the redesign and we are in the process of rolling out an updated version of our mobile apps.
on February 18, 2026, our Board of Directors declared a quarterly dividend of $1.99 per common share payable on March 30, 2026 to shareholders of record at the close of business on March 13, 2026.
we expect that our capital expenditures will be approximately $120 million in 2026.

Under our December 2024 agreement which expires in December 2029, our U.S. supplier agreed to provide the Company with an uninterrupted supply of cheese and the Company agreed to purchase substantially all of its U.S. pizza cheese from this supplier during the five-year term of the contract.

The majority of our meat toppings in the U.S. come from a single supplier under a contract which was renegotiated in December 2025 and expires at the end of December 2027, subject to a one-year optional extension.

catalysts.event: Coca-Cola exclusive beverage supplier contract minimum purchase milestone
catalysts.sentence: This contract, renegotiated in December 2023, provides for Coca-Cola to continue to be our exclusive beverage supplier and expires on December 31, 2030 or at such time as a minimum number of cases of Coca-Cola products are purchased by Domino's, whichever occurs later.

tamClaims.claim: The U.S. QSR pizza category grew from $42.8 billion to $43.4 billion from 2024 through 2025.
tamClaims.sentence: From 2024 through 2025, the U.S. QSR pizza category grew from $42.8 billion to $43.4 billion and is primarily comprised of dine-in, delivery and carryout, with delivery and carryout comprising the two largest segments.
